findings.title: React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182 / CVE-2025-66478) — Next.js / React RSC Pre-Auth RCE — Original PoC Now Public
findings.summary: The original researcher behind CVE-2025-55182 (React2Shell, CVSS 10.0) has published their full PoC for the pre-auth RCE in React Server Components, triggering a new wave of mass-scanning tooling including a Google Tsunami scanner plugin, with multiple independent PoC repositories and confirmed ongoing exploitation by China-nexus threat actors; all Next.js applications with Server Actions or RSC enabled and running pre-patch versions require immediate patching.

findings.title: TeamPCP CanisterWorm — Self-Propagating npm Supply Chain Attack Expands to 135 Malicious Artifacts
findings.affected_technology: npm ecosystem (Node.js / JavaScript), GitHub Actions CI/CD
findings.summary: The TeamPCP threat actor has expanded their supply chain attack beyond the Trivy GitHub Actions compromise into the npm ecosystem, deploying a self-propagating worm across 135 malicious package artifacts in 64+ packages under @emilgroup and @teale.io namespaces, which installs a persistent Python backdoor polling an ICP blockchain canister as C2 and autonomously harvests npm publish tokens to spread to additional packages, exposing any CI/CD pipeline or developer environment running npm install to credential theft and persistent backdoor.

noted.summary: Critical vulnerability reported as potentially exploited in attacks against education sector; insufficient public technical detail available this cycle.
Use-after-free in libfuse's io_uring I/O path disclosed March 21, 2026; local privilege escalation vector on Linux systems running FUSE filesystems with io_uring, no remote vector confirmed.
NULL dereference in libfuse's io_uring I/O path disclosed alongside CVE-2026-33150; local-only impact, no remote vector confirmed.
Information disclosure flaw exposing user email addresses in base64-encoded OAuth state parameter (CVSS 5.3); patched via plugin update March 9, 2026, not exploited in wild.

Reflected XSS via error_description parameter with Safari onpagereveal WAF bypass (CVSS 6.1), chainable with CVE-2026-31381 for PII harvest to active exploitation; server-side fix applied March 6, 2026.
Buffer overflow in DTLS cookie callback when callback returns cookie exceeding DTLS1_COOKIE_LENGTH; fixed in pyOpenSSL 26.0.0 released March 15, 2026.
Insecure Direct Object Reference allowing unauthorized access to PII, discovered by GitHub Security Lab; patch status and CVSS not yet available.

Threat actors abuse Azure Monitor alert descriptions to send legitimate SPF/DKIM/DMARC-passing callback phishing emails from azure-noreply@microsoft.com; novel infrastructure-abuse technique with no fix available.
